question_title: Will WHO Disease Outbreak News publish a new mpox-related item before May 1, 2026?
summary: WHO Disease Outbreak News has published 2 mpox-related DONs in the 162 days since the PHEIC was lifted (DON587 on Dec 5, 2025 and DON595 on Feb 14, 2026). A Bayesian Poisson model gives ~64% base probability of at least 1 new DON in the 66-day resolution window (Feb 24-May 1, 2026). Modest upward adjustment reflects: ongoing multi-country epidemic with 46,000+ clade I cases, active surveillance of a novel recombinant strain (only 2 known cases), European outbreaks, and potential for country-specific DONs. Offsetting: 14 days of the window have passed with no DON, the window is slightly shorter than the 71-day observed inter-DON interval, and only 2 post-PHEIC data points make the rate estimate uncertain.

factors.description: Bayesian Poisson base rate from post-PHEIC data: 2 DONs in 162 days gives ~64% mean probability of at least 1 DON in a 66-day window
Novel recombinant strain (clade Ib/IIb) with only 2 known cases under active global genomic surveillance — any new detection would likely trigger a DON update, though partially captured in base rate
Ongoing multi-country epidemic: 46,000+ clade I cases, European outbreaks with local transmission, continued multi-clade spread — country-specific DONs are additional possibilities
Post-PHEIC DON frequency (2 in 5 months) shows DONs remain an active publication channel for mpox, resuming after a gap during the PHEIC period
14 days of window elapsed with no new DON; 66-day window is slightly shorter than the observed 71-day inter-DON interval

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.74 (74%)**

Key reasoning:
- **Base rate**: Post-PHEIC data shows 2 mpox DONs in 162 days. A Bayesian Poisson model gives ~64% probability of at least 1 DON in the 66-day window.
- **Upward factors**: Active multi-country epidemic (46,000+ cases), novel recombinant strain under global surveillance, potential for country-specific DONs (like Sweden/South Africa in 2024).
- **Downward factors**: 14 days of window passed without a new DON; the window (66 days) is slightly shorter than the observed 71-day interval between the two post-PHEIC DONs; only 2 data points make the rate uncertain.
- **Net**: More likely than not that WHO will publish at least one new mpox DON before May 1, but not near-certain given the modest post-PHEIC publication rate.
sources_consulted: [Mpox: recombinant virus with genomic elements of clades Ib and IIb – Global situation](https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2026-DON595)
[(Metaculus) Will WHO Disease Outbreak News publish a new mpox-related item before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42508)
[Broader transmission of mpox due to clade Ib MPXV – Global situation](https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2025-DON587)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if WHO Disease Outbreak News publishes at least one item on or after February 24, 2026 and before May 1, 2026 whose title contains “mpox” or whose content is primarily about mpox. This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is the WHO Disease Outbreak News listing at https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news.
fine_print: Use the publication date shown on the WHO item page. If classification disputes arise, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: WHO Disease Outbreak News RSS feed (backup navigation, if available) https://www.who.int/feeds/entity/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/en/rss.xml | WHO mpox topic page (backup cross-check only) https://www.who.int/health-topics/mpox
background_info:
This forecasts whether WHO will issue a new outbreak notice about mpox soon, relevant for global health monitoring and preparedness.
